Interactive rhythm pathway

ECG Rhythms Step by Step

Identify the earliest diagnostic checkpoint using the R–C–P method, then name the rhythm and choose the emergency response.

2 strips available
ECG RHYTHMS STEP BY STEP

R → C → P Method

Start with rhythm and rate, then assess the QRS complex, and finally use the P wave when it still adds diagnostic value.

Each strip reveals its questions sequentially. A step becomes dark grey only when proceeding to it adds no diagnostic value.
1
R–C–P pathway

Strip 1

Three-stage rhythm recognition and immediate emergency response.

Expert wide-complex pathway

Strip 2

Seven stages covering diagnostic level, P-wave value, presentation, causes, differential diagnosis, and emergency management.

ECG RHYTHMS STEP BY STEP

Strip

R Rhythm & Rate
C QRS Complex
P P Wave

Emergency-treatment logic follows the 2025 AHA adult tachyarrhythmia and cardiac-arrest algorithms. Always assess the patient, pulse, and haemodynamic state—not the monitor alone.
